without.result_preview:
Here's a comprehensive trace of how NestJS dependency injection works:

---

## NestJS Dependency Injection: Full Trace

### 1. `@Injectable()` — Marking a Class

`packages/common/decorators/core/injectable.decorator.ts:43-48`

When you annotate a class with `@Injectable()`, it calls `Reflect.defineMetadata()` to stamp two pieces of metadata onto the class:
- `INJECTABLE_WATERMARK = true` — marks it as a managed provider
- `SCOPE_OPTIONS_METADATA` — stores the scope (SINGLETON/REQUEST/TRANSIENT)

with_pruner.result_preview:
Here's a full trace of how NestJS DI works, from decorator to resolved instance:

---

## Phase 1: Metadata Registration (`@Injectable()`)

**`packages/common/decorators/core/injectable.decorator.ts:43`**

`@Injectable()` is a simple class decorator that stamps two metadata keys onto the class:

```ts
Reflect.defineMetadata(INJECTABLE_WATERMARK, true, target);   // marks it as a provider
Reflect.defineMetadata(SCOPE_OPTIONS_METADATA, options, target); // stores scope (DEFAULT/REQUEST/TRANSIENT)

**`integration/hello-world/src/timing.interceptor.ts`** — new interceptor that:
- Records `Date.now()` before the handler runs
- Uses `tap()` on the response observable to log after the response completes
- Logs `METHOD /path — Xms` via NestJS `Logger`

**`app.module.ts`** — registered `TimingInterceptor` as a global interceptor via `APP_INTERCEPTOR`, which applies it to every route in the application without needing `@UseInterceptors` on individual controllers.

Here's a full trace of how NestJS DI works, based on the actual source:

---

## Phase 1: Decoration — Setting Metadata

**`@Injectable()` — `packages/common/decorators/core/injectable.decorator.ts`**

When you decorate a class with `@Injectable()`, it calls `Reflect.defineMetadata()` to set two entries:
- `__injectable__` → `true` (marks it as a provider)
- `scope:options` → `{ scope, durable }` (Singleton/Request/Transient)

**TypeScript + `reflect-metadata`** automatically emits `design:param

with_pruner.result_preview:
Here's how the NestJS DI system works end-to-end:

---

## 1. `@Injectable()` — Decorator Phase (compile time)

`injectable.decorator.ts:43`

```ts
Reflect.defineMetadata(INJECTABLE_WATERMARK, true, target);
Reflect.defineMetadata(SCOPE_OPTIONS_METADATA, options, target);
```

It stamps two pieces of metadata onto the class:
- `INJECTABLE_WATERMARK` — marks it as a provider Nest can manage
- `SCOPE_OPTIONS_METADATA` — stores the scope (`DEFAULT`, `REQUEST`, or `TRANSIENT`)
